spec_match.rationale: Exposes the spec's interest-target-show-delay and interest-target-hide-delay CSS properties as sliders and reflects the resulting hover behaviour live — exactly the surface the spec adds.

interactive.rationale: Two range sliders (show delay, hide delay) plus an 'accidental-hover scenario' button drive a real popover with a styled hover target so authors can feel the delays.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature blurb sells 'drag the sliders and feel what each delay does' — page implements exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: Show + hide delays covered, with an explicit 'accidental hover' scenario. Missing: keyboard-focus path (delays apply there too), touch long-press default delay, and the cascade where individual element vs ::interest-target pseudo-style sets different delays.

references.rationale: The see-also list does NOT include https://chromestatus.com/feature/4530756656562176. Design-system rule 3 requires every page to embed the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link for THIS feature. Without it the fix-slugs.py self-heal can't recover the folder if the slug ever drifts.

design_system.rationale: Range sliders use accent-color via CSS variables; the 'Run scenario' button overrides background+color together (rule 6); no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: CRITICAL: add the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link
openQuestions.detail: The see-also list omits https://chromestatus.com/feature/4530756656562176. Sibling concepts interestfor-attr and link-previews include it; this one (and form-field-help, input-mode-matrix, rich-tooltips) do not. Design system rule 3 requires it everywhere. Add the link.

openQuestions.title: Show keyboard-focus delays alongside hover delays
openQuestions.detail: Keyboard users also pay the show/hide delays — but with no analog of mouse leave/return. A focus-only second target with the same sliders would show that the delays still apply but the dismiss path differs (blur happens immediately on Tab away).

openQuestions.title: Annotate the spec defaults on the sliders
openQuestions.detail: The CSS spec lists default values for interest-target-show-delay and interest-target-hide-delay. The sliders default to 300/200ms but it's unclear if those are the SPEC defaults or arbitrary. Mark the spec's default value with a tick on each slider so authors know which side of the default they're tuning.

summary: Strong delay-tuning playground that exposes the right spec surface, but it misses the canonical chromestatus link (rule 3 violation). Fix that, add keyboard-focus alongside hover, and annotate the spec defaults on the sliders.
